title: Connector params: compiler
analysis:
Task 241 requires the compiler to: (1) create synthetic __connector_N sub-components when a connector type is specified in a connect statement, (2) compile parameter assignments from the connector body, (3) generate port compatibility constraints, and (4) generate frame alignment constraints when both ports satisfy LocatedPort.

All four requirements are ALREADY IMPLEMENTED in compile_connection() at lib.rs:3773-4034:
- Lines 3903-3966: Synthetic sub-component creation (__connector_N) with compiled params, content hash, best-effort param validation against connector template
- Lines 3888-3901: Compatibility constraint generation (connect_compat_{left}_{right}) as boolean literal
- Lines 3992-4022: Frame alignment constraint generation via trait_satisfies() check for LocatedPort (including transitive refinement)
- Lines 3305-3327: Content hash computation includes all connection components (ports, operator, mappings, connector_sub, frame_constraint)

21 existing tests in connect_compile_tests.rs all pass, covering: basic connections, connector sub-component creation, chain desugaring, direction errors (forward/reverse/bidi), port mapping propagation, unknown port errors, content hash sensitivity (type, params, operator, frame), port type mismatch warnings, trait refinement, connector param validation, frame alignment with LocatedPort, frame alignment via refinement chain, and frame_constraint=None for non-LocatedPort.

Gaps identified: (1) No test for connector with BOTH params AND port mappings combined, (2) No test for multiple connectors on same entity verifying __connector_0/__connector_1 indexing, (3) No test verifying connector args can reference parent scope parameters. These edge cases should be tested to ensure robustness.

steps.description: Write test `connector_with_params_and_port_mappings` verifying that a connect statement with BOTH params and port mappings produces: (a) a connector sub-component with correct structure_name and compiled args, (b) port_mappings propagated to CompiledConnection, (c) no errors. Source: `connect a -> b : BoltSet { grade = 10.9  shaft -> bore }`
Verify step-1 test passes against existing implementation (compile_connection already handles both params and port_mappings independently; this test confirms they work together). Fix if needed.

Write test `multiple_connectors_per_entity` verifying that two connect statements with connectors in the same structure produce __connector_0 and __connector_1 with distinct names and correct structure_names. Source: two connect statements with different connector types (BoltSet and RivetSet).
Verify step-3 test passes against existing implementation (connector_index is tracked in ConnectAccumulator and incremented per connector). Fix if needed.

Write test `connector_args_reference_parent_params` verifying that connector parameter expressions can reference the parent structure's params. Source: `structure def S { param g : Real = 8.8 ... connect a -> b : BoltSet { grade = g } }`. Assert the connector sub-component's args contain a CompiledExpr with a ValueRef to the parent param.
Verify step-5 test passes against existing implementation (compile_expr uses ctx.scope which includes parent params). Fix if needed.
Run full compiler test suite (`cargo test -p reify-compiler`) and verify all tests pass including the 21 existing connect_compile_tests plus the 3 new edge-case tests.
Final verification: ensure no regressions across the full compiler test suite. All 24+ connect tests and all other compiler tests must pass.

design_decisions.decision: No new implementation code needed — core connector compilation is already complete
design_decisions.rationale: compile_connection() at lib.rs:3773-4034 already handles all four task requirements: synthetic __connector_N sub-components (lines 3903-3966), parameter compilation (3908-3913), compatibility constraints (3888-3901), and frame alignment constraints (3992-4022). 21 tests verify this behavior. The remaining work is edge-case test coverage.

design_decisions.decision: Connector sub-components use Private visibility and __connector_N naming
design_decisions.rationale: Connectors are implementation details of the connection, not user-visible members. Private visibility prevents external access. The __connector_N naming uses a monotonically incrementing index within each entity to ensure unique names, matching the __count_N and __guard_N synthetic naming conventions.

design_decisions.decision: Frame alignment constraint is a boolean literal (true) placeholder
design_decisions.rationale: The current implementation emits frame_align_{left}_{right} constraints as Bool(true) literals. This establishes the constraint infrastructure and content hash dependency. The actual frame transformation logic (comparing frame expressions from both ports) will be implemented in a later task when the evaluator supports spatial transforms.

design_decisions.decision: Connector param validation is best-effort (only when connector template already compiled)
design_decisions.rationale: The compiler processes entities in source order. If the connector structure appears after the entity using it, its template won't be available yet for param validation. The current implementation gracefully skips validation in this case via the `ctx.compiled_templates.iter().find()` guard at line 3917.

reuse.how: Connector params are compiled using the same compile_expr() used for all other expression compilation, with the parent entity's CompilationScope. This means connector params can reference parent params, lets, and port members.

Frame alignment detection reuses the existing trait_satisfies() function which performs transitive refinement checking through the trait registry. This means ports typed with MechanicalPort (which refines LocatedPort) automatically get frame constraints.
Connector sub-component hash combines connector type + operator + port names + param expression hashes using the same ContentHash::combine/of_str pattern used throughout the compiler.

Synthetic connector sub-components reuse the same SubComponentDecl struct as regular sub-components (params, type_args, is_collection, count_cell, content_hash), just with Private visibility and auto-generated name.
All new edge-case tests reuse the existing test helper functions that parse source text and compile to CompiledModule or (TopologyTemplate, Vec<Diagnostic>).
